title: The Shower Principle

jokes.text: I started eating the lettuce on my plate under my onion rings
jokes.setup: Liz claiming she's had a year of major changes
jokes.punchline: Her idea of major dietary change is eating lettuce that was already on her plate
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Liz's delusional self-improvement mindset revealed through absurdly minimal change

jokes.text: I'm actually eating the onion part of my onion rings
jokes.setup: Previous year's version of the same conversation
jokes.punchline: Even more pathetic version of the dietary 'improvement' - now she's proud of eating the actual onion ring
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the pattern - shows how even her pathetic changes get more pathetic year over year

jokes.text: thanks to my exciting relationship with Carol, who is a man. Although we have done nothing that lesbians could not do
jokes.setup: Mentioning her relationship with Carol
jokes.punchline: The clarification that Carol is a man followed by the TMI about their sex life
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific oversharing with a perfectly absurd sexual detail that's both TMI and confusing

jokes.text: thanks to my boyfriend Wesley, whom I hate
jokes.setup: Mentioning positive changes thanks to a boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that she hates him
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character insight - the casual hatred delivered as throwaway information

jokes.text: Every morning I get up and come to this office while my wife cheats on me with Jared
jokes.setup: Howard defending predictability as not necessarily bad
jokes.punchline: His example of predictability is his wife's affair
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - the accountant's deadpan delivery of devastating personal information as a mundane example

jokes.text: I remember Mrs. Doyle pulling me aside to talk about it in the seventh grade
jokes.setup: Liz asking if Jack is familiar with 'the shower principle'
jokes.punchline: Jack's mind immediately goes to a sexual talk from a teacher
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on Jack's privileged private school background and dirty mind

jokes.text: I was in the shower when I finally got the title of the movie Face off. Face off, face off.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining the shower principle with a personal example
jokes.punchline: His big insight was understanding the ridiculously obvious title 'Face/Off'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - his intellectual pretension applied to solving the most obvious movie title pun

jokes.text: Nerd alert, nerd alert
jokes.setup: Jack's lengthy scientific explanation
jokes.punchline: Liz's childish response to actual science
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character beat - Liz's anti-intellectual response to Jack's showoff explanation

jokes.text: To sit on their couches, and hear stories. Here's one. 1968. We were on patrol somewhere near the Cambodian border.
jokes.setup: Hank explaining Kabletown's simple goal to entertain people
jokes.punchline: Immediately launches into traumatic Vietnam War story
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character establishment - Hank's folksy demeanor immediately undercut by dark war trauma

jokes.text: So we boiled the skull, and made a tea kettle out of it
jokes.setup: The war story continuing
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of making tea from a human skull
jokes.explanation: Horrifically dark comedy delivered with perfect folksy casualness - shows Hank's disturbed psychology

jokes.text: Everyone hide your bones. Dogbeard the pirate just sailed into woof harbor
jokes.setup: Dog fashion show announcement
jokes.punchline: Terrible dog puns in the show description
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad corporate humor - works as meta-joke about terrible TV writing

jokes.text: Because I think Corka-Coola brand diet banana lime causes 'tum nungnuss'
jokes.setup: Asking for real soda instead of the cheap brand
jokes.punchline: Both the knockoff name and the made-up medical condition
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist wordplay - both the fake brand name and invented medical term land well

jokes.text: we got a 'jeers' in Corporate Blimps Weekly
jokes.setup: Jack complaining about the Kabletown blimp being in disrepair
jokes.punchline: The existence of Corporate Blimps Weekly magazine and getting negative coverage
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist detail - the hyper-specific trade publication and 'jeers' as opposite of 'cheers'

jokes.text: a white man who still buys Cadillacs
jokes.setup: Jack describing the challenge of convincing Hank
jokes.punchline: Reducing Hank to this very specific demographic stereotype
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's casual stereotyping and class consciousness

jokes.text: Whoa, gumballs!
jokes.setup: Liz declaring she won't do something comically undignified
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets distracted by gumballs and presumably does something undignified
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback - Liz's complete lack of self-awareness as she proves Jack's point instantly

jokes.text: Cut it out, you perverts. I know what you're doing. It's the first warm day of the year, and women are starting to take off their winter clothes
jokes.setup: Liz addressing the male staff
jokes.punchline: Her clinical explanation of seasonal male behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Liz's awkward way of addressing sexual tension in the workplace

jokes.text: Eyes down here, boys. I have breasts, you know.
jokes.setup: Hazel trying to get attention from the men
jokes.punchline: Her desperate and backwards attempt at seduction
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for Hazel - her awkward desperation and complete misunderstanding of seduction

jokes.text: Button up. Cerie, put on one of my soup ponchos
jokes.setup: Liz trying to control the spring clothing situation
jokes.punchline: The existence and implied appearance of Liz's 'soup ponchos'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character detail - 'soup ponchos' perfectly captures Liz's practical, unstylish wardrobe choices

jokes.text: Take a picture, it'll last longer. Here you go.
jokes.setup: Cerie using the classic sarcastic retort
jokes.punchline: Someone actually taking a picture
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expectation - taking the cliché literally for a visual gag

jokes.text: because her lines didn't have any 'K' sounds, which she thinks is the funniest sound
jokes.setup: Explaining why Jenna accused Liz of sabotage
jokes.punchline: Jenna's ridiculous theory about K sounds being inherently funny
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna absurdity - her narcissistic pseudo-scientific theory about comedy delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: My cousin Karl crashed his car, and now he's in a coma at the Kendall clinic
jokes.setup: Liz demonstrating Jenna's K sound theory
jokes.punchline: Making up a tragic story filled with K sounds
jokes.explanation: Brilliant example - proves the absurdity by creating a dark story that's 'funny' only because of alliteration

jokes.text: But I just bought a Letterman jacket with 'the German' embroidered on it. It's not refundable if you personalize it!
jokes.setup: Liz warning Pete not to force a nickname this year
jokes.punchline: Pete has already bought a jacket with his self-given nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pete pathetic-ness - his desperate attempt at coolness backfiring in the most expensive way

jokes.text: You never say that name in the theater. It is curs-ed.
jokes.setup: Jenna reacting to mention of Macbeth
jokes.punchline: Her theatrical pronunciation of 'cursed' as two syllables
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jenna's theatrical affectations and superstitions

jokes.text: I was trained in stage acting and game show pointing at the Royal Tampa Academy of dramatic tricks
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining her theatrical credentials
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous fake institution and curriculum
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jenna moment - combines her delusional self-importance with absurdist institution naming

jokes.text: Where I studied theater superstition, under Professor Duane 'Crawfish' Kenny
jokes.setup: Continuing her fake credentials
jokes.punchline: The absurd professor name and specialty in 'theater superstition'
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up absurdism - 'Crawfish Kenny' and making superstition an academic subject

jokes.text: a timely satire of MacBeth, where mayor McCheese and his wife, an ambitious pickle, murder king Ronald
jokes.setup: Liz explaining the sketch isn't actually Macbeth
jokes.punchline: McDonald's characters performing Shakespeare
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist concept - combining high culture Shakespeare with corporate McDonald's characters

jokes.text: Do you know how many mirrors I've smashed just thinking it was a blonde woman mocking me?
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining she doesn't need more bad luck
jokes.punchline: Her narcissistic paranoia leading to property damage
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Jenna's narcissism and paranoia creating a perfectly absurd neurosis

jokes.text: I just have to be out in time for my band to be on Letterman
jokes.setup: Cerie agreeing to fill in for Jenna
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning her band will be on Letterman
jokes.explanation: Good Cerie moment - her casual mention of impossible success that's probably real

jokes.text: I bought a groupon for a couples' massage at a resort in the Catskills where Henny Youngman used to take his mistresses
jokes.setup: Hazel asking Liz out
jokes.punchline: The hyper-specific and inappropriate resort history
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail showing Hazel's inappropriate oversharing and weird romantic planning

jokes.text: As the doctor said to me after my hepatitis test, 'you got it, sweetheart.'
jokes.setup: Hazel agreeing to help Liz
jokes.punchline: Using a hepatitis diagnosis as a casual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark misdirection - seems like agreement then reveals disturbing medical information

jokes.text: I don't trust my accountant. I think he's lying to me about being Jewish. I step on more wine glasses than he does.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he does his own taxes
jokes.punchline: His metric for authentic Jewishness
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy logic - his completely wrong understanding of Jewish wedding traditions

jokes.text: I was gonna tell Dotcom I loved his novel as an April fool's joke
jokes.setup: Tracy realizing it's April
jokes.punchline: His cruel April Fools joke targeting Dotcom's artistic dreams
jokes.explanation: Dark but character-appropriate - Tracy's casual cruelty toward his friend's aspirations

jokes.text: I went and took a shower at the Racquet Club, where I saw Lou Dobbs step on his own testicles
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why he looks weird
jokes.punchline: The horrifying and impossible visual of Lou Dobbs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist visual - impossible anatomy combined with specific celebrity creates unforgettable image

jokes.text: Still trying to get the ol' jumper cables on the tires of your brain muffler?
jokes.setup: Liz trying to use a car metaphor for Jack's thinking
jokes.punchline: Completely mixing up car parts in nonsensical ways
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - her attempt at clever metaphor revealing complete ignorance

jokes.text: You have to talk like this! You can only say what's essential! And you get to point at things!
jokes.setup: Jack reminiscing about GE factories
jokes.punchline: His romantic view of industrial communication limitations
jokes.explanation: Good Jack moment showing his romanticized view of blue-collar work despite being completely out of touch

jokes.text: 'Summer horse grave'?
jokes.setup: Liz showing off her meditation candle
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous scent name on the candle
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist product name - disturbing and nonsensical scent description

jokes.text: Meditation is a waste of time, like learning French, or kissing after sex
jokes.setup: Jack dismissing meditation
jokes.punchline: His list of 'useless' activities revealing his personality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - his elitist yet emotionally stunted worldview in one line

jokes.text: thanks to his frequent scratch-off lotto victories, finding all that treasure on the island he owns in the Caribbean
jokes.setup: Explaining Tracy's increased income
jokes.punchline: Mixing mundane lottery wins with absurd treasure hunting
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy absurdism - combining realistic and fantastical income sources matter-of-factly

jokes.text: And designing Kate Middleton's wedding gown. She was wonderful. Collaborative, fun, and a little dangerous.
jokes.setup: Continuing Tracy's income sources
jokes.punchline: Tracy designing royal wedding dresses and his assessment of Kate
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist continuation - Tracy's impossible side career and unexpected royal gossip

jokes.text: Five dog now five. No, that's just the confusing title Disney gave the fifth Snow dogs movie. The 'S' s are fives.
jokes.setup: Tracy saying nonsense movie title
jokes.punchline: The explanation of Disney's ridiculous numbering system
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist wordplay - satirizing corporate sequel naming with genuinely confusing result

jokes.text: I took all of my money and put it in my pool and froze it
jokes.setup: Explaining why he's not liquid despite being wealthy
jokes.punchline: His literal interpretation of liquid assets
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy logic - taking financial terminology literally in the most impractical way possible

jokes.text: Like Lady McCheese, I will 'screw my courage to the sticking McNugget.'
jokes.setup: Jenna deciding to do the sketch despite superstitions
jokes.punchline: McDonald's version of the famous Macbeth quote
jokes.explanation: Excellent literary parody - maintaining the rhythm of Shakespeare while making it absurdly corporate

jokes.text: a state of pure inner peace, where all pants have built-in underwear
jokes.setup: Describing transcendent enlightenment
jokes.punchline: Liz's enlightenment is about practical clothing solutions
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Liz's 'spiritual' enlightenment is about mundane comfort

jokes.text: Is it, 'time to make the donuts'?
jokes.setup: Jack tries to get Liz's mantra
jokes.punchline: Guesses Dunkin' Donuts slogan as her spiritual practice
jokes.explanation: Good character joke - Jack assumes Liz's spirituality would be food-related. Fits both characters perfectly.

jokes.text: Is it, 'time to make the donuts'?
jokes.setup: Asking about Liz's mantra
jokes.punchline: Reference to old Dunkin Donuts commercial
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference playing on Liz's food obsessions and blue-collar mentality

jokes.text: Buy me a drink first. Ow.
jokes.setup: Jack saying only Liz can stimulate his brain
jokes.punchline: Liz's sexual interpretation and Jack's immediate rejection
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing the awkward sexual undertones in their relationship

jokes.text: shower me, Lemon. Shower me in the inane waters of television, food, and feet
jokes.setup: Jack realizing Liz is his shower principle
jokes.punchline: His poetic description of Liz's obsessions as inspiration
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Jack's pretentious language applied to Liz's most mundane concerns

jokes.text: I once pantsed Deepak Chopra while Craig T. Nelson taped it
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why he doesn't meditate
jokes.punchline: His specific celebrity humiliation story
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist celebrity name-dropping - specific enough to be hilarious, random enough to be Jack

jokes.text: It can't be done. Oh, God, the carpet. Somebody hold my hair back.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to drink a gallon of milk
jokes.punchline: His immediate failure and vomiting
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out physical comedy but works as payoff to earlier setup

jokes.text: I've seen people killed with shoes, but never like that
jokes.setup: Reacting to Jenna's shoe injury
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning she's witnessed multiple shoe murders
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark Hazel moment - her violent past revealed through helpful commentary

jokes.text: like I need another curse. Too busty.
jokes.setup: Jenna lamenting the Macbeth curse
jokes.punchline: Her narcissistic interpretation of being 'cursed' with beauty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism - turning superstition into self-compliment

jokes.text: My casserole's burning! My casserole's burnt!
jokes.setup: Jack trying to meditate and needing distractions
jokes.punchline: His frantic domestic concerns while trying to achieve enlightenment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's inability to let go of control even while meditating

jokes.text: You know what they'd thought when I saw up your skirt. Nice. Someone won the crotch jackpot.
jokes.setup: Hazel revealing she caused the accidents
jokes.punchline: Her inappropriate sexual comment about Jenna
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Hazel's inappropriate behavior and obsession

jokes.text: sing Motown together into hairbrushes, have a pair of vintage Jordache jeans that we share
jokes.setup: Hazel explaining her friendship goals with Liz
jokes.punchline: Her specific and creepy vision of female friendship
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hazel's twisted romantic view of friendship

jokes.text: I can't afford a third strike
jokes.setup: Jenna asking if Hazel tried to kill her
jokes.punchline: Hazel's casual reference to her criminal history
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy revealing Hazel's criminal past through practical consideration

jokes.text: you made the same mistake Mickey Rourke made on that catamaran
jokes.setup: Jenna threatening Hazel
jokes.punchline: Her random and mysterious Mickey Rourke reference
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna absurdity - threatening someone with unexplained celebrity story

jokes.text: You made the same mistake Mickey Rourke made on that catamaran.
jokes.setup: Jenna responds to Hazel's threats
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical reference to a specific celebrity boat incident
jokes.explanation: Great absurd specificity - Jenna's random celebrity reference as strategic wisdom is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: If I get thirsty, I'll just drink the water from lunch I saved in my cheek
jokes.setup: Hank rejecting bottled water as too fancy
jokes.punchline: His disturbing alternative water storage method
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Hank's folksy persona taken to gross extreme

jokes.text: If I get thirsty, I'll just drink the water from lunch I saved in my cheek.
jokes.setup: Jack offers Hank bottled water
jokes.punchline: Hank stores water in his mouth like a chipmunk
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Hank's extreme frugality taken to absurd biological level. The casual delivery makes it perfect.

jokes.text: Taking my original buttocks wasn't enough for those bastards
jokes.setup: Hank mentioning Vietnam making couches
jokes.punchline: His personal Vietnam trauma involving his buttocks
jokes.explanation: Dark brilliant character moment - specific and disturbing war trauma delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Taking my original buttocks wasn't enough for those bastards.
jokes.setup: Hank mentions Vietnam making furniture
jokes.punchline: Personal grudge about stolen body parts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark character detail - Hank's war experience reduced to petty property concerns about his 'original buttocks.'

jokes.text: I fell asleep at a Raymour and Flanigan last week, and a black family tried to buy me
jokes.setup: Hank comparing himself to a couch
jokes.punchline: His story of being mistaken for furniture
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Hank's self-deprecating story with subtle racial element

jokes.text: Not buying the Phillies, not marrying my wife sooner, and not surprising you any quicker with this hug
jokes.setup: Hank listing his three life regrets
jokes.punchline: The increasingly personal and immediate nature of the regrets
jokes.explanation: Good escalating structure moving from major life decisions to immediate physical affection

jokes.text: so I said I had a bomb
jokes.setup: Tracy's heated conversation with the treasury department
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate escalation of the tax dispute
jokes.explanation: Good dark escalation showing Tracy's complete lack of understanding about consequences

jokes.text: Liz and I, or 'Lazel,' are really worried about you
jokes.setup: Hazel trying to manipulate the situation with Jenna
jokes.punchline: Her self-created couple name with Liz
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Hazel's delusional relationship with Liz

jokes.text: You're just another weird page, and I already have one of those. Aw, thank you.
jokes.setup: Liz dismissing Hazel as just another weird page
jokes.punchline: Kenneth taking this as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Kenneth's positive interpretation of everything

jokes.text: Jack, the rats have my meditation stool. Help! Shoot it free! Let her go, you bastard!
jokes.setup: The factory setting with rats
jokes.punchline: Liz's dramatic reaction to rats stealing her stool
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy and callback - Liz's dramatic relationship with her meditation equipment

jokes.text: It is a tail told by an idiot, full sound of furry, signifying nothing
jokes.setup: Kenneth reciting Macbeth
jokes.punchline: His mispronunciation turning it into animal puns
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing Kenneth's earnest but flawed cultural knowledge

jokes.text: Would you like to try our apple dippers?
jokes.setup: Kenneth finishing the profound Shakespeare soliloquy
jokes.punchline: Immediately switching to fast food sales mode
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Kenneth's seamless transition from high culture to service industry

tmdb_overview: A curse strikes TGS. Liz dares to be different after realizing that the same things happen every year, and Jack vows to wow Hank Hooper.
insight: Absurdist density peaks at 89.2 with 69 jokes across 42 minutes of controlled chaos.
